item.title: Australia to remain in talks on action in the Strait of Hormuz as ceasefire takes effect
item.description: Defence Minister Richard Marles says discussions will continue about Australia's involvement in ensuring the Strait of Hormuz remains open.

item.title: US architect Rex Heuermann admits to murder of eight women in Gilgo Beach case
item.description: Rex Heuermann pleads guilty to seven murders and admits to killing another woman, solving the notorious case known as the Gilgo Beach killings.

item.title: The Australian Ballet and Bangarra Dance Theatre unite for Flora after failed referendum
item.description: Sixteen dancers from Bangarra Dance Theatre and 19 ballet dancers from the Australian Ballet have joined forces for Flora, choreographed by Bangarra artistic director Frances Rings.

item.title: TV quiz: Match the quote to the classic Australian television show
item.description: One quote, two possible answers: test your knowledge and take a nostalgic trip down memory lane as you match the quote to the iconic Australian television series.

item.title: How the legacy of Australia's Black Power movement continues today
item.description: When a "bunch of nobodies" in Sydney decided to stand up to police brutality, no-one could guess their impact would still be felt more than 50 years later.

item.title: As Trump's Iran rhetoric escalates, Australia turns to the art of diplomatic understatement
item.description: Neither side of politics in Australia rushed to enthusiastically celebrate the ceasefire deal that followed US President Donald Trump's threat.

item.title: How long will it take petrol prices to get back to pre-Iran war levels, if at all?
item.description: Iran's promise to reopen the Strait of Hormuz after striking a temporary agreement with the US saw the cost of oil fall sharply. But how quickly are we likely to see relief at the bowser in Australia? Analysts say it could be weeks or months.

item.title: Anthony Albanese dashes to Singapore as Australia engages in a costly, complex fuel scramble
item.description: Anthony Albanese is flying to Singapore as part of a hastily arranged regional mission to shore up crucial fuel supplies, amid warnings the global energy crisis could "sharply turn for the worse".

item.title: Mining on the Moon the next space race in the bid to explore the final frontier
item.description: A scientist, an engineer, an archaeologist and a lawyer break down some of the physical, environmental, ethical and legal challenges of mining on the Moon.

item.title: 'Ketamine Queen' jailed for 15 years over death of Friends star Matthew Perry
item.description: Jayvee Sangha admits to running a "stash house" that helped supply the famous Friends star ketamine in the months before his death.

item.title: NT government urged to release 'secretive' review into Kumanjayi White's death
item.description: The family of Indigenous man Kumanjayi White has criticised the secrecy around the independent review into his death, which has been handed to NT Police and the DPP but not released publicly.

item.title: Farmers furious as Victorian government fast-tracks caged hen rule changes
item.description: The lives of caged hens are set to improve as Victoria implements new welfare laws, but farmers say they have not been consulted.

item.title: Brisbane introduces 'tall over sprawl' strategy in bid to meet housing demand for next 15 years
item.description: As Brisbane prepares for an expected surge in population over the next 15 years, the council has introduced a plan to allow high-rise apartments in some of the city's suburban hubs.

item.title: Public pledges sought for Justice For Her to help spark 'actions' to reduce gendered violence rates
item.description: A new platform, dubbed Justice For Her, is centred on making small, actionable pledges to help people overcome the overwhelming statistics on gendered violence, which often lead to inaction.

item.title: Severe Cyclone Maila moving again towards Far North Queensland after stalling in Solomon Sea
item.description: Cyclone Maila has woken up and is heading west, with the category four system still likely to reach Queensland's far north on Monday.

item.title: Camperdown couple 'relieved' to see home relocated after being built on wrong block
item.description: A Victorian couple has been forced to move their home that was accidentally built on the wrong block.

item.title: Neuroscientist slams AFL's 'potentially dangerous' headgear move
item.description: The AFL has backed a new helmet that aims to reduce the risk of concussion, but a top neuroscientist is calling it "bad scientific practice".

item.title: FOI reveals extent of Adelaide Writers' Week crisis, including locked doors and threats to staff
item.description: An extraordinary series of emails sheds light on the reservations that preceded, and internal fallout that followed, the decision to scrap Randa Abdel-Fattah's Writers' Week appearance.

item.title: Iran and US already at odds over ceasefire agreement after Israel bombs Lebanon
item.description: Israeli strikes on Lebanon threaten to scuttle the US-Iran ceasefire as Iranian state media reports the Strait of Hormuz has been closed.

item.title: Fuel price hikes force families to reconsider travel for medical treatment
item.description: Paige Edgar never imagined the hardest part of her one-year-old son's brain cancer diagnosis would be getting him to treatment five hours from her home.

item.title: AC Milan, Inter Milan and Juventus to play matches in Perth
item.description: Perth will be the centre of Italian preseason football in August, with some of the game's heavyweights heading to the Western Australian capital ahead of the next European season.

item.title: Police investigate fatal shooting of man at Box Hill in Melbourne's east
item.description: Homicide squad detectives from Victoria Police are investigating a fatal shooting at a Box Hill apartment building.

item.title: Calls to review Australian food labelling laws as farmers fight cheap imports
item.description: Farmers battling cheap imported produce want a review of food labelling laws to highlight what Australian produce is included in products.

item.title: Australians dust off bikes, buy trikes as fuel prices surge amid Iran war
item.description: Skyrocketing fuel prices are driving Australians to pedal power, with some bicycle stores and repair services experiencing a surge in demand.

item.title: UK man who sexually abused girl has visa reinstated over 'strong ties' to Australia
item.description: The man's permanent visa was automatically cancelled when he was jailed in 2024 for sexually abusing a nine-year-old girl, but a tribunal has upheld his appeal in part due to his "ill health".

item.title: Nick Faldo says he will not comment on rival Greg Norman until after US Masters
item.description: Respectfully biting his tongue, Nick Faldo is refusing to dignify fresh criticism from Greg Norman that is certain to reignite one of golf's fiercest rivalries.

item.title: Darwin's 'dangerous' bus network will soon have officers armed with guns
item.description: As the conflict in the Middle East continues, more people than ever are turning to public transport to escape the cost of driving — but in Darwin, that choice comes with a different price.

item.title: Daniil Medvedev smashes racquets, loses 6-0, 6-0 to Matteo Berrettini at Monte Carlo Masters
item.description: Daniil Medvedev endures an eventful day at the Monte Carlo Masters, smashing several racquets before suffering the first 6-0, 6-0 trouncing of his distinguished career.

item.title: Punmu's future at stake as damaged road leaves remote community at 'crisis point'
item.description: A remote Aboriginal community in Western Australia has six weeks' worth of food remaining and no truck driver is willing to travel down the notorious road to reach it.

item.title: WA food charities inundated as fuel crisis sends more people to their doors
item.description: From shrinking their hampers to closing waitlists, food charities in Western Australia are adapting their services to meet a surge in demand as more people struggle with rising costs.

item.title: Rolling 2026 AFL All-Australian team 1.0: Max Gawn tracking towards league record as Will Ashcroft headlines eight first-timers
item.description: Melbourne captain Max Gawn shows no sign of slowing down as an excellent first month of the season puts him front and centre of ABC Sport's rolling AFL All-Australian team.

item.title: Melbourne Comedy Festival: Grace Jarvis on comedy as an autistic artform, chronic pain and working in a sex shop
item.description: Grace Jarvis talks about everything from her experiences with autism, ADHD, queerness, and working in a sex shop in her stand-up. Her experiences with chronic illness? Not so much.

item.title: More than 1,000 fish killed in Darwin Port after iron ore pollution
item.description: More than 1,000 small fish die in Darwin Port after acidic run-off from an iron ore stockpile enters a containment pond following heavy rain.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz ship traffic remains at standstill despite Iran, US and Israel ceasefire
item.description: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has remained at an effective standstill in the 24 hours since Iran conditionally lifted its blockade on the critical shipping lane, amid a fragile ceasefire with the US and Israel.

item.title: NT government in talks with Daly River residents, landowners over potential relocation of Nauiyu
item.description: Flood-affected residents from Daly River are in talks with the NT government about moving the remote Aboriginal community to higher ground, with a church offering to donate land for the township's relocation.

item.title: Fire damages roof of velodrome used at 2016 Rio Olympic Games
item.description: Rio de Janeiro's Olympic velodrome has been damaged following a fire, which required about 80 firefighters and 20 fire trucks to bring under control.

item.title: China's influence on the US-Iran ceasefire deal
item.description: China's Foreign Minister Wang Yi says he held 26 phone calls with his counterparts in Iran, Israel, Russia and Gulf countries before the ceasefire was reached.

item.title: Geraldton man charged with murder after death of 69yo man sparks homicide investigation
item.description: A man dies in hospital after an altercation at a home in Padbury, and police have charged a 39-year-old man who was arrested at the scene with murder.

item.title: Australian playwright S Shakthidharan wins $250,000 Windham-Campbell Prize
item.description: S Shakthidharan, whose plays include The Wrong Gods, The Jungle and the Sea and Counting and Cracking, has won the prestigious Windham-Campbell Prize.

item.title: Tommy Fleetwood's son steals show at US Masters Par 3 Contest
item.description: The annual Par 3 Contest on the eve of the US Masters has produced four aces, but Tommy Fleetwood's eight-year-old son stole the show at Augusta National.

item.title: Donald Trump criticises NATO over Iran in meeting with alliance's boss
item.description: US President Donald Trump vents his frustration with NATO during a private meeting with its secretary-general as relations in the military alliance reach a crisis point over the Iran war.

item.title: Woman charged after allegedly attacking man with hammer in Royal Prince Alfred Hospital
item.description: Viki Graham has been remanded in custody after allegedly attacking a man with a hammer at a Sydney hospital overnight.

item.title: Rain falls in Adelaide for AFL Gather Round, as interest shown for second event in NSW, Queensland
item.description: AFL fans are facing a weekend of wet weather for Gather Round as the South Australian Premier says he is "not going to rush" negotiations on the future of the event.

item.title: SA heavy vehicle freight reforms fast-tracked to allow heavier, longer trucks
item.description: The South Australian government says recent changes to heavy vehicle freight limits will lower fuel costs and reduce the number of freight trucks needed on the road.

item.title: Tintinara farm owner convicted over clearing 112 hectares of bushland
item.description: The owner of a farm in South Australia's south-east is convicted and fined for clearing 112 hectares of bushland, but the penalty is much less than the $2.2 million he would have had to pay for a permit for the work.

item.title: Tom Duffy to start for Brisbane Broncos, Adam Reynolds ruled out of Cowboys clash
item.description: Brisbane captain Adam Reynolds has succumbed to injury and will not play against North Queensland.

item.title: People living in vehicles forced to choose between diesel and food as fuel prices remain high
item.description: The high cost of fuel is making it even more difficult for people living in their cars to afford food, electricity and transport.

item.title: Four people to face court for alleged offences 'connected' to murder of Adelaide father Chris Robertson
item.description: Four people have been arrested by police for alleged offences "connected" to the alleged murder of Adelaide father-of-two Chris Robertson last year.

item.title: $1 million reward for information on unsolved death of Harmony Bryant
item.description: Police hope a $1 million reward will produce fresh information about the death of a 26-year-old Kyogle woman, who was found suffering severe burns in bushland on the NSW Mid North Coast.

item.title: $10m Winx filly Quinceanera won't race, but co-owner has 'no regrets'
item.description: An owner says she has "no regrets" after announcing that the first filly from champion race mare Winx will never race.

item.title: Winx's daughter, Quinceanera, at trackwork
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Victorian opposition promises 200 more PSOs to patrol railway stations across Melbourne
item.description: The Victorian opposition is promising to recruit hundreds more protective service officers if it wins government in November.

item.title: Bruce Lehrmann's bid to appeal defamation case ruling refused by High Court
item.description: The High Court was Bruce Lehrmann's last legal avenue to challenge a Federal Court ruling that found, on the balance of probabilities, he did rape Brittany Higgins.

item.title: Wests Tigers looking to win three NRL matches in a row for the first time since 2018
item.description: The last time Wests Tigers won three matches in a row, Ivan Cleary was still the club's coach and Benji Marshall had just begun his comeback as a player. Now, after eight years of waiting, they have the chance to end that streak.

item.title: Lebanese fear war could get 'much worse' as Israel attacks amid fragile ceasefire
item.description: As a shaky ceasefire struggles to get under way, deadly Israeli strikes on Lebanon risk worsening the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Nitrous oxide a 'significant contributing factor' in Canberra teenager's drowning death, coroner finds
item.description: An 18-year-old was heavily impaired by nitrous oxide, known as "nangs", when his car entered a pond, prompting a coroner to call for a review of the gas's availability in the ACT.

item.title: Fremantle convenience store crackdown to curb 'under the counter' illegal nicotine sales
item.description: The City of Fremantle voted unanimously at Wednesday's council meeting to amend local planning laws, in a bid to stop more convenience stores that sell illicit tobacco products from opening.

item.title: What makes a relationship de facto and how can it impact a break-up?
item.description: Our "ordinary understanding" of de facto relationships may not match up with the legal definition and that can cause big surprises for people in the event of a break-up.

item.title: Gang rapist on the run after escaping custody at Sydney hospital
item.description: A man convicted of gang raping a 14-year-old girl in a Western Sydney park has escaped from custody while at a hospital. Follow live.

item.title: Jury retires to consider verdict in Irma Palasics murder trial after weeks of evidence
item.description: Justice David Mossop told the jury it had a "complex" and "substantial" task after the court was presented with 53 witnesses, 14 statements or recordings, and 97 exhibits.

item.title: Matt Canavan's rebrand takes shape in sunglasses, Albanese rebukes Trump
item.description: With retro shades, big rhetoric and a rejection of net zero, Matt Canavan is hoping to reshape the Nationals. His first big test is just weeks away.

item.title: Lance Collard to learn fate over alleged homophobic slur after marathon AFL tribunal hearing
item.description: Lance Collard may face 10 weeks on the sidelines if found guilty of using a homophobic slur, after an AFL disciplinary tribunal held over its verdict until Friday.

item.title: Police calling for witnesses after wanted man left with life-threatening injuries
item.description: Police allege the 34-year-old "was seen to be acting suspiciously" near homes at Bray Park near Murwillumbah when he was confronted.

item.title: King Charles praises Territorians, warns of climate change dangers in open letter on 'devastating' NT wet season
item.description: King Charles has written an open letter to Northern Territory residents following recent devastating flooding, saying the "increasingly catastrophic effects of climate change are surely a siren-call to the world for more urgent action".

item.title: Thursday finance with David Chau
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Crows get home against Blues in the wet in Gather Round opener
item.description: The Adelaide Crows send the fans home happy after ending its losing streak by beating Carlton at a packed Adelaide Oval to start Gather Round. Follow live.

item.title: Emperor penguins, Antarctic fur seals now endangered due to climate change
item.description: The iconic emperor penguin and the Antarctic fur seal are edging closer to extinction, according to a projection by the world's largest environmental network.

item.title: Bulldogs stun misfiring Panthers in big boilover victory
item.description: The Penrith Panthers unbeaten start to the NRL season has been ended in comprehensive fashion by the Bulldogs at Stadium Australia.

item.title: Alcoa rejects concerns over increased mercury levels at its Wagerup refinery
item.description: The US mining giant says increased mercury emissions from its Wagerup refinery are a by-product of higher production, amid a Conservation Council appeal against its operating licence.

item.title: Police investigation underway after officer pushes Indigenous woman to the ground during arrest in Broome
item.description: An internal police investigation is underway, after video footage emerged online showing a police officer pushing a woman to the ground in Western Australia's far north.

item.title: Man charged with murder over Sunshine Coast woman's 'suspicious' kangaroo crash
item.description: NT police have charged a 33-year-old man with murder over the "suspicious death" of his female partner, who was found dead on an outback road over the weekend, after he alleged she was injured in a crash involving a kangaroo.

item.title: ADF chief says surveillance data not contributing to war effort but warship could if asked
item.description: Australia's chief of defence says Australia could "absolutely" deploy a warship to the Strait of Hormuz if asked by the federal government.

item.title: Combatting concussion in contact sport
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Weekly news quiz: A historical orbit, emotional support animals and a tiny outback town
item.description: It's been a big week in the news. Did you catch these stories?

item.title: David Hallberg is leading the Australian Ballet into the future with passion and drive
item.description: The Australian Ballet helped put David Hallberg back together after a grave injury. He now returns that favour and leads our national ballet company with a new verve and energy.

item.title: Do you ever need to throw out your old bath towels?
item.description: Expert tips on getting the most out of your favourite bath towels and when it might be time to replace them.

item.title: How the PM's Singapore visit could help Australia's fuel supply
item.description: Experts say visits like Anthony Albanese's trip to Singapore are important for securing Australia's energy supplies. As one observer puts it: "When you don't have the stock, you need friends."

item.title: Illegal and legal wildlife trade fuelling animal-to-human disease transmission, report finds
item.description: Of the 2,079 mammal species traded worldwide, 41 per cent share at least one pathogen with humans, according to new data released today.

item.title: Construction sector warns of housing delays, mass lay-offs due to Middle East war
item.description: Contractors, business owners and economists warn that thousands of construction industry businesses could go bust as a result of supply chain disruptions caused by the Iran War, unless the government intervenes.

item.title: TelstraSuper closes Direct Access investment product without properly notifying members
item.description: A superannuation investment option spruiked as offering "choice and flexibility" has been shut down without proper notification, leaving members less than two months to sell all their assets.

item.title: The NDIS's 'social licence' isn't just at risk of eroding – it's close to collapse
item.description: The NDIS's reputation isn't just at risk of coming under pressure, it's well and truly crumbling, write Nas Campanella and Evan Young.

item.title: Taylor and Canavan are chalk and cheese – and that's a problem for Taylor
item.description: If you want a basic measure of the difference between Liberal leader Angus Taylor and Nationals leader Matt Canavan, compare their reactions to US President Donald Trump's extraordinary threat this week that "a whole civilisation will die tonight".

item.title: Racism erupts after Melbourne announces Little India plans, but some Indians say it will build connection
item.description: Social media posts about the announcement of a Little India precinct in Melbourne are flooded with racist comments, leaving some Indians with mixed feelings about the plan.

item.title: Goalkeeper Andrew Redmayne marks 300th A-League game on home turf
item.description: His career began as a substitute for Mark Bosnich almost 20 years ago, now Andrew Redmayne is joining the A-League's exclusive 300 club.

item.title: Police arrest three people over fatal shooting of man at Box Hill tower
item.description: A 47-year-old Bonnie Brook man has been charged over the alleged murder of a man in a high-rise apartment building in Melbourne's east.

item.title: Jog for Joe aims to raise awareness of male eating disorders in Australia
item.description: Joe Cappello was 25 years old when he died after a 12-year struggle, and the statistics show many more men are at risk.

item.title: Teenage boy charged over Ipswich crash that killed passenger
item.description: A teenage driver has been charged with dangerous driving after a crash that killed his 16-year-old passenger.

item.title: Cyclone Maila weakens to category two as it slowly heads to Queensland
item.description: Cyclone Maila has been downgraded to a category two system, pushing back the expected time it will make landfall to Tuesday.

item.title: Sexual misconduct findings permanently added to health practitioners' records
item.description: More than 100 past and current practitioners have had findings of sexual misconduct added to their public-facing records under new reforms to promote transparency.

item.title: Adelaide GP Mark Rogers disqualified for professional misconduct amid 'blatant disregard' for previous orders
item.description: An Adelaide doctor with an "unhealthy obsession" for low-level laser therapy has had his registration cancelled after a tribunal found he demonstrated a "blatant disregard" of previous orders imposed on his registration.

item.title: Three Tasmanian MPs share their experiences with mental health and politics
item.description: A national study has found 70 per cent of politicians feel their job is affecting their mental health. The ABC spoke to three Tasmanian MPs, including the state's premier, about their experiences.

item.title: Safety review links herb in popular cold and flu supplements with life-threatening anaphylaxis
item.description: The TGA says people taking popular cold and flu supplements face the risk of life-threatening anaphylaxis from a herbal ingredient.

item.title: NSW drug diversion scheme disproportionately sends Aboriginal people to court, report says
item.description: A BOSCAR report finds NSW Police disproportionately used "discretion" to reject eligible low-level drug offenders from a diversion program — especially Aboriginal people.

item.title: Tiwi Island birder hopes rare photos of endemic owl encourage conservation
item.description: A traditional owner on a Northern Australian island has captured rare photos of an "enchanting" masked owl, adding to the limited knowledge base surrounding the endemic and culturally significant bird.

item.title: Masters 2026: Jason Day among early leaders, 2025 winner Rory McIlroy returns to top of leaderboard
item.description: Jason Day kicks off his Masters tilt with a 3-under opening round, while 2013 champion Adam Scott is among a host of players to card eagles on day one at Augusta.

item.title: How the Parkes Dish is helping track Artemis II on its historic journey
item.description: The Parkes Observatory — also known as The Dish — continues a legacy of supporting lunar missions that started more than 50 years ago, helping Artemis II go where humanity has not since 1972.

item.title: Australian role in Hormuz depends on permanent ceasefire, Marles says — as it happened
item.description: Australia has been involved in talks about assisting the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz but any action can only take place if a ceasefire becomes permanent, Richard Marles says.

item.title: Study finds Tasmanian native forest logging increases potential for more severe bushfires
item.description: Scientists have studied satellite images of a Tasmanian bushfire and found regrowth from extensive logging and recent bushfires has "absolutely" increased the risk of more severe bushfires.

item.title: Southern Queensland farmers take pre-emptive action as dry spell stretches on
item.description: Queensland farmers gripped by a dry spell are turning to drastic and inventive measures: offloading most of their livestock and laying kilometres of water pipelines.

item.title: Low-sensory voting to be introduced statewide at 2026 Victorian election
item.description: State election voting will be made quieter and calmer for neurodivergent voters in Victoria.

item.title: Mark Gregory's mum just wants to know what happened to her son 35 years ago
item.description: Mark Gregory was just 28 when he was killed by a hit-and-run driver while walking home from a party in 1991. His family just wants answers.

item.title: ASX 200 and Aussie dollar down, Brent crude and WTI oil up — as it happened
item.description: The Australian market has finished the trading week on a disappointing note, dropping slightly, despite US stocks rising overnight. Look back on our business blog.

item.title: Search for missing Murwillumbah woman Yvonne Whear continues in northern NSW
item.description: The daughter of Yvonne Whear, 73, who went missing in February, says there is no evidence to suggest her mum left her northern NSW home of her own accord.

item.title: Truck driver charged over Sunshine Coast crash that killed three-year-old girl
item.description: A truck driver has been charged with drug driving over a crash that killed a three-year-old girl and critically injured her one-year-old sister.

item.title: Cheat Sheet: Charting Euphoria's long and messy road to season three
item.description: Sam Levinson's controversial teen series has had to overcome four years of actor deaths, character departures and on-set tension to make it to season three.

item.title: Alex de Minaur sets up Monte Carlo Masters quarterfinal clash with local hero Valentin Vacherot
item.description: Monegasque star Valentin Vacherot is next on the docket for Australian Alex de Minaur after an impressive win over rising star Alexander Blockx.

item.title: Aussie rock band DMA'S on performing at the AFL Gather Round
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Man charged after $1.1 million of diesel fuel allegedly stolen from unmanned Queensland depots over 12 months
item.description: Police will allege a 48-year-old man used stolen fuel cards to obtain the diesel from unmanned depots over a 12-month period.

item.title: Hills Shire Mayor leads calls to simplify governance of Hawkesbury River
item.description: The Hawkesbury-Nepean River flows through eight NSW council areas and is looked after by 13 agencies, which one mayor says leads to confusion about who is in charge of what.

item.title: Chimpanzees made "territorial patrols'
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Chimpanzee's in Uganda's Kibale National Park fight
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Chimpanzees in Uganda's Kibale National Park wage lethal 'civil war'
item.description: A decades-long study reveals hundreds of chimpanzees waging a violent and deadly conflict against their former friends in their remote forest home.

item.title: Penrith Panthers player Casey McLean faces suspension for high tackle in loss to Canterbury
item.description: Penrith centre Casey McLean is facing a one-week suspension for a high shot that left Canterbury prop Max King with a broken jaw.

item.title: Rainy weather easing for AFL Gather Round weekend matches, as Barossa welcomes fans
item.description: The Bureau of Meteorology says wet weather conditions will improve for AFL Gather Round matches as the weekend progresses, as the premier shares his ongoing bid to keep the event in South Australia.

item.title: Pilbara residents launch petition after warning system confusion during cyclone
item.description: Residents in the Pilbara raise concerns over the state's warning system for cyclones following ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle last month.

item.title: UK man who sexually abused girl has visa reinstated over 'strong ties' to Australia
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Family of NSW surfer Guy Haymes deliver victim impact statements
item.description: Guy Haymes was left in a vegetative state after an assault during a dispute over $50 in February, 2024.

item.title: Why the Iran war fuel crisis will get worse before it gets better
item.description: The official pledges of the last few days may have placated markets at first, but they have become little more than bargaining chips ahead of this weekend's negotiations between the US and Iran.

item.title: Seal sleeping on road causes Mornington Peninsula traffic delays
item.description: Locals say they are not surprised the seal, described as a regular visitor, decided to stop for a snooze on the roadway.

item.title: Cyclone Maila batters Solomon Islands as it moves through PNG towards Far North Queensland
item.description: Roads and buildings have been destroyed across multiple Pacific islands as slow-moving Tropical Cyclone Maila heads towards Papua New Guinea, with the eye of the storm set to cross over two remote islands early tomorrow.

item.title: Australia vs Great Britain Billie Jean King Cup: Talia Gibson and Kimberly Birrell lose singles
item.description: Australia's quest to win a place in the Billie Jean King Cup Finals is off to the worst possible start after losses in both opening-day singles matches.

item.title: What to watch and listen to this weekend
item.description: An old favourite, new Courtney Barnett and a tasty Korean vegetarian stew.

item.title: Artemis II crew about to make splash landing on Earth after journey around Moon
item.description: The Orion space capsule will enter the Earth's atmosphere as a burning fireball, travelling at about 40,000 km/h, splashing down off the coast of southern California. Here's a breakdown of how the spacecraft copes with high temperatures, slows down, and what happens as it splashes down.

item.title: Molly Picklum to face fellow Australian Isabella Nichols in Bells Beach semifinal
item.description: Molly Picklum wants to ring the bell but will need to topple defending champion and countrywoman Isabella Nichols after the pair starred again in the World Surf League's season opener.

item.title: Afrika Bambaataa, rap icon accused of sexual abuse, dead at 67
item.description: The Universal Zulu Nation founder was a beloved hip hop icon before sexual abuse and trafficking allegations surfaced in the 2010s.

item.title: At the site of an Israeli strike in Beirut, locals say 'there's no Hezbollah here'
item.description: In a residential area in Beirut an entire building had been razed to the ground and a nut shop was destroyed. The locals told the ABC "there's no Hezbollah here".

item.title: Man pleads guilty to dealing with millions in cash from illegal tobacco sales
item.description: Malek Alhmedai's hire car had $4 million stashed inside, with the money linked to illegal tobacco sales.

item.title: Australia's economy is underpinned by housing, which is why housing won't get cheaper, super boss warns
item.description: AustralianSuper boss says housing prices unlikely to fall much, because major banks can't afford them too

item.title: Commuters trapped in South Eastern Freeway gridlock blast lack of signage
item.description: Commuters stuck queuing for up to four hours following a truck crash on the South Eastern Freeway on Thursday blast the transport department over a lack of signs warning them of the road closure ahead.

item.title: Brazil cricket player Laura Cardoso takes nine wickets in T20 international to set new record
item.description: Brazilian cricketer Laura Cardoso has set a new record that is going to be tremendously hard to beat.

item.title: Anthony Albanese and Singapore's prime minister talk trade, fuel in bilateral meeting
item.description: Australia and Singapore have agreed to make "maximum efforts" to meet each other's fuel and gas needs as the crisis in the Middle East continues, with the prime minister securing an encouraging but not legally binding deal.

item.title: Taiwan's opposition leader Cheng Li-wun has met Xi Jinping in China for the first time
item.description: Taiwan's opposition leader has done something for the first time in a decade, and experts say it could shape relations between Beijing, Taipei and Washington.

item.title: Cowboys claim thrilling Queensland derby victory with last-minute try
item.description: The North Queensland Cowboys have won a thriller at Lang Park, beating the Brisbane Broncos 35-31. Earlier, the Dragons lost their sixth-straight to start the 2026 NRL season.

item.title: Man jailed for stomping on head of off-duty police officer in Newtown attack
item.description: Koby Weaver is jailed over his role in what is described as a "brutal" attack which left an off-duty police officer with serious injuries.

item.title: Friday finance with David Chau
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Dunsborough Primary School left scrambling to pay $130k water bill
item.description: Dunsborough Primary School is racking up massive water bills in comparison to nearby schools, to the detriment of student support and repairs to facilities.

item.title: Dockers launch late fightback to pip Magpies in frantic finish
item.description: Fremantle wins its fourth match in a row after surviving a tense final few minutes against Collingwood at Adelaide Oval.

item.title: Australian War Memorial display on Ben Roberts-Smith altered as refurbished sections unveiled
item.description: As the Australian War Memorial unveils some of its newest elements as part of a $500 million redevelopment, director Matt Anderson confirms it has altered a display dedicated to Ben Roberts-Smith in the wake of his arrest for alleged war crimes.

item.title: Court releases vision of attack on off-duty officer
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: WA Police say bones found on Rottnest Island are historic Indigenous remains
item.description: Several human bones that were found on Rottnest Island this week are historic Indigenous remains, WA Police say.

item.title: NT Police investigating death of child found in Palmerston driveway
item.description: Northern Territory Police are investigating the death of an infant who was found inside a vehicle parked in the driveway of a Palmerston home on Friday.

item.title: St Kilda forward Lance Collard guilty over using homophobic slur
item.description: St Kilda forward Lance Collard has been found guilty of using a homophobic slur against an opponent, leaving his AFL career hanging in the balance.

item.title: Lachlan Kennedy breaks 10-second barrier in 100m, with national record in his sights
item.description: Lachlan Kennedy has scorched to a sub-10-second run in the heats of the 100 metres at the Australian Athletics Championships, and promises his best is still to come.

item.title: US Vice-President JD Vance heads to Pakistan for Iran peace talks
item.description: The centre of Islamabad has been locked down as US delegates, including JD Vance, travel to Pakistan for peace talks with Iran.

item.title: UK charity Sentebale sues Prince Harry for defamation
item.description: Prince Harry co-founded Sentebale 20 years ago in honour of his late mother, Princess Diana, but now the organisation is suing him for defamation.

item.title: Trump, Netanyahu and how war in Iran is reshaping US politics
item.description: Extraordinary reporting reveals how Israel made the case for regime change in Iran — and how the fallout is driving a profound shift in American opinion, amid deep uncertainty about what the war has achieved.

item.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: This week we're testing your knowledge of a classic Aussie poem, oyster recipes and Itchy and Scratchy
item.description: Every question has only two options: one is right, and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

item.title: Debra Adelaide reflects on friendship with Gabrielle Carey in When I Am Sixty-Four
item.description: Debra Adelaide became friends with Gabrielle Carey when they were 12. After Carey died by suicide five decades later, Adelaide tried to address her unanswered questions in a novel.

item.title: Cosplay is rising in popularity. What are the rules for dressing up?
item.description: Cosplayers say their hobby has had an astronomical rise in popularity over the past decade. While the subculture promotes "no limits" creativity, there are still strict rules.

item.title: Albanese's petro-diplomacy can't overcome the pain of what may lie ahead
item.description: This is not over. The hard stuff is only just beginning. And it's going to get bumpy, testing the nation's resilience and its political leadership.

item.title: Israel admits it did not expect Iran would close the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: How Australia is navigating an oil market in crisis
item.description: Australia is stitching together a steady supply of fuel through a patchwork of relationships. But how long can that last?

item.title: Philippine music takes global stage as P-pop groups secure gigs at major US festivals
item.description: As one of the world's largest music festivals gets underway this weekend, a pop group from the Philippines has made history.

item.title: Iran uses sea mine threat to 'maintain chokehold' over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran claims to have littered a central shipping passage in the Strait of Hormuz with sea mines, in a move analysts say could be a "coercive threat" to maintain its grip on the crucial waterway.

item.title: Trump threatens Iran with warships ahead of 'make-or-break' talks — as it happened
item.description: US Vice-President JD Vance is due to arrive in Pakistan as both sides level accusations of ceasefire breaches.

item.title: War hero to accused war criminal: Inside Ben Roberts-Smith's prosecution
item.description: Once celebrated as Australia's most decorated living war hero, the looming prosecution of Ben Roberts-Smith is shaping up to be the next trial of the century.

item.title: Israel's ambassador concedes Iran's chokehold on Strait of Hormuz was not expected before war
item.description: Israeli ambassador Hillel Newman admits his country was surprised by the scope of Iran's response to the Middle East war and that it did not expect Gulf states to be targeted in retaliation strikes.

item.title: Jess Hull and Claudia Hollingsworth speak after the dramatic women's 1,500m final
item.description: Sport news, live scores, features, analysis and photos from the world of sport. Comprehensive coverage of AFL, cricket, football, rugby league and rugby union. Audio interviews. News stories and highlights in video. Sports news bulletin.

item.title: Jess Hull crashes out and Claudia Hollingsworth disqualified in dramatic women's 1,500m final
item.description: Jess Hull had been on track to claim a fourth-straight 1,500m national title before being clipped by Claudia Hollingsworth, bringing her reign crashing down.

item.title: Expansion on cards at Eromanga refinery as Queensland looks to produce more oil
item.description: The Inland Oil Refinery produces specialised underground mining fuel, heating oils and other speciality solvents, and supplies a small amount of on-road diesel for the local community

item.title: Milk prices may need to increase as dairy farmers hit hard by fuel and fertiliser costs
item.description: Dairy farmers across Australia are battling crippling fuel and fertiliser costs. The industry is now calling for a 30-cent-per-litre rise in milk prices on supermarket shelves.

item.title: What to do if you get a fishhook injury and how to avoid one
item.description: What do you do when the hook catches you instead of the fish? Charter fishers and a medical expert weigh in.

item.title: Greyhound trainers frustrated over 'lack of accountability' as safety issues continue at Ipswich facility
item.description: Ipswich facility The Q was promised to be "as safe for dogs and trainers as possible", but several dogs died during its trial period and events have been cancelled due to unsafe conditions.

item.title: Alex de Minaur suffers painful Monte Carlo quarterfinal loss to Monaco's Valentin Vacherot
item.description: Alex de Minaur will slip from his career-high ranking after missing a chance to emulate his semifinal run at the Monte Carlo Masters, beaten in three sets by Monaco's Valentin Vacherot.

item.title: Renewed push for completion of dumped Victorian Murray Basin freight rail project
item.description: North-west Victorian farmers are pushing for the completion of the Murray Basin Rail Project, more than seven years after it was originally slated to be finished.

item.title: Long-range goals and their impact on the 2026 AFL season
item.description: In a league where inside-50s are so often king, teams with prowess from outside the arc are making a major impact to start the 2026 AFL season.

item.title: Locals fear Jamisontown church will be bulldozed to build new homes
item.description: Residents on a suburban street in Western Sydney fear 130 years of history will be erased if a 19th century church is bulldozed to build new homes.

item.title: More modern buildings being added to SA heritage list, as owners raise concerns
item.description: More modern buildings are being added to South Australia's state heritage list, especially churches, but some owners object to their properties being preserved, worried that it will add cost and complexity to their plans.

item.title: Live: Astronauts hoisted to recovery ship after 'perfect bullseye' landing
item.description: The crew on board the Orion has returned to Earth, splashing down in the Pacific Ocean in a perfect '"bullseye". Follow live.

item.title: Police appeal for information about mysterious shooting death of Richard Wills at Ouyen
item.description: Richard Wills was found shot and buried in a shallow grave at his rural property in Victoria's north-west earlier this week.

item.title: British man who avoided deportation after child sex offence could face fresh visa review
item.description: A British child sex offender who avoided deportation is facing a possible visa review, after an ABC investigation.

item.title: Armidale museum freezes dozens of koalas for taxidermy and research
item.description: Most people usually drive past animals killed on the road, but in Armidale, they stop, bag them up, and drop them off at the local museum.

item.title: Rotting sperm whale carcass forces relocation of Tasmania's largest open water swim
item.description: A 40-tonne sperm whale carcass has forced the relocation of Tasmania's biggest open water swim. Organisers of the event had hoped it would be moved, but the state's environment department says that was not feasible.

item.title: Tamworth teen Max Lye is no chicken in the egg business
item.description: While many teenagers spend their spare time online gaming or playing sport, 14-year-old Max Lye hangs out with a bunch of chooks and dreams of owning his own egg farm.

item.title: Plans to move residents off Cocos (Keeling) Islands walked back after backlash
item.description: After seething criticism from residents, the Commonwealth has released its final plan to protect the Cocos (Keeling) Islands from rising sea levels.

item.title: Only a fraction of donated items are of good enough quality to resell, so this charity fixes
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Hobart City Mission repairing donated clothing to reduce textile waste
item.description: Every year, lots of new textile items are imported to the Australian market … about 1.55 billion of them. Fast fashion is a leading contributor to overproduction and makes reuse difficult.

item.title: Cyclone Maila weakening and slowing as it heads towards Australia
item.description: After causing damage to islands in the Pacific, Cyclone Maila is downgraded as it heads towards Australia.

item.title: Cost-of-living pressures drive outback students to train as teachers locally
item.description: Ashlee Toms and Aria Miller graduated from high school and walked back through the school gates as staff less than six months later.

item.title: Rory McIlroy holds record Masters lead after stunning second round, Jason Day bogeys 18th hole at Augusta
item.description: Jason Day does not do a lot wrong in the second round at Augusta National, but Rory McIlroy is looking just about untouchable after two rounds of the Masters.

item.title: Fuel crisis sees less motorists on Sydney roads but small rise in public transport use, data shows
item.description: It is exactly six weeks since the conflict began in the MIddle East and data suggests Sydney motorists have changed how they travel in order to cope with record fuel prices.

item.title: Lance Collard's extraordinary AFL disciplinary hearing sets new precedent
item.description: In an AFL first, St Kilda's Lance Collard has faced a disciplinary tribunal hearing for conduct unbecoming.

item.title: By rejecting his bid for appeal the High Court has made Bruce Lehrmann's defamation case a complete failure
item.description: The High Court was Bruce Lehrmann's last legal avenue to challenge a Federal Court ruling that, on the balance of probabilities, he raped Brittany Higgins — now that avenue has closed.

item.title: Mobile ministry in the Parish of Avon
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Dedicated priest travels countryside for services despite shrinking congregations
item.description: Anglican priest David Perryman delivers Communion to a congregation of one in a small town about three hours east of Melbourne — but not for much longer.

item.title: Cyclone Vaianu forces Supercars to cancel third and final Taupō race
item.description: A looming cyclone forces organisers to cancel the Supercars race in Taupō on New Zealand's north island.

item.title: Kakadu helicopter crash victim recounts dramatic rescue on CareFlight's 20th anniversary
item.description: In the aftermath of a 2019 helicopter crash in Kakadu National Park, Fred Hunter lay on the ground covered in blood and fuel, when he felt something biting his leg. On the 20th anniversary of CareFlight, Mr Hunter recounts his dramatic rescue by the service.

item.title: Two people left with 'traumatic' injuries after violent home invasion in Melbourne's north, police say
item.description: Victoria Police says three or four males in their late teens or early 20s wore masks when they broke into a Greenvale home.

item.title: This rich, meat-free curry is a budget-friendly way to make a massaman - ABC News
item.description: Mushrooms are a cost-effective way to make a hearty massaman that is also vego and vegan-friendly.

item.title: Highland cattle breeder numbers climb in WA as interest spreads and agritourism grows
item.description: Registered Highland cattle breeders have more than tripled in Western Australia in the past five years. The shaggy Scottish breed is gaining popularity across WA, with more breeders interested in taking on the cattle and strong demand from agritourism.

item.title: Live: Bombers snap club-record losing streak, Lions hold off Roos
item.description: Essendon ends a 17-game losing streak, while defending premiers Brisbane withstand a big challenge from North Melbourne ahead of a top-four clash between the Swans and Suns at Norwood. Follow live.

item.title: Artemis II crew takes first steps on recovery ship, the USS John P Murtha
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Thailand's enormous fishing industry highlights 'big trouble' caused by Middle East war
item.description: Tens of thousands of small and commercial fishing vessels in Thailand are stranded on shore, no longer able to afford diesel, which has more than doubled in price since the Iran war began.

item.title: NT police calling for information after man found dead near road in Tennant Creek
item.description: The Northern Territory Police Force is calling for information from the public after a man was found dead near a roadway in the remote town of Tennant Creek.

item.title: Live: Raiders roar to life after dynamic first half
item.description: After four straight losses the Raiders have found some of the football that helped take them to last year's minor premiership. Follow live.

item.title: Karoline Leavitt refuses to comment on US support for Israel's offensive in Lebanon
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Donald Trump speaks about an Israeli invasion of Lebanon
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Israel's mass killing has forced Donald Trump to think about Lebanon
item.description: For weeks, Israel has been intensifying its deadly strikes across Lebanon, and Donald Trump has had little to say. But now he has been forced to make it a priority.

item.title: Three people die in vehicle crash in Stoneville
item.description: Three people have died after a vehicle left the road and hit a tree in the Perth Hills suburb of Stoneville.

item.title: Man's body found after Acacia Ridge house fire in Brisbane's south
item.description: Emergency services were called to a house fire on the corner of Foote and Annear streets in Acacia Ridge, where a man's body was found.
